skill.name: code-reviewer
skill.description: Guides AI assistants through structured code reviews for pull requests, implementations, and code snippets. It focuses on quality, security, performance, testing, and documentation.
skill.summary: Structured code review guidance for quality, security, performance, and maintainability.
skill.author: DennisLiuCk

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged shell execution, weak cryptography, and reconnaissance patterns, but these are false positives from Markdown text and review examples. No executable scripts, network calls, credential access, prompt injection attempts, or malicious intent were found in SKILL.md.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Code Fence Reported as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The external command finding at line 82 points to a Markdown code fence that starts a Python example. It is documentation, not executable skill logic, and no shell command execution is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Line 82 is the opening of a fenced Markdown example. The surrounding lines show a SQL injection teaching example, not runtime command execution.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Review Guidance Reported as Weak Cryptography
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alerts point to descriptive prose about code review guidance and design patterns. There are no cryptographic algorithms, hashes, ciphers, or security-sensitive implementations at those lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain plain-language skill descriptions and review criteria. I found no evidence of weak cryptographic code or instructions.

low_findings.title: False Positive: SQL Injection Example Reported as Reconnaissance
low_findings.description: The low-severity reconnaissance alerts point to an educational SQL injection example that contrasts unsafe and safe query construction. The content teaches reviewers to identify SQL injection and does not instruct system reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines are SQL query examples inside a documented security review example. There is no system enumeration, host discovery, or data exfiltration behavior.

content.user_title: Review Code for Quality and Security
content.value_statement: Code reviews can miss security, performance, and maintainability issues when feedback is unstructured. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through consistent, actionable reviews.
actual_capabilities: Reviews code for readability, naming, complexity, and duplication.
Checks for common security issues such as SQL injection, XSS, and exposed secrets.
Evaluates performance risks including inefficient queries, memory leaks, and missing caching.
Assesses test coverage, edge cases, and test maintainability.
Produces review feedback organized by summary, strengths, issues, recommendations, and code suggestions.
limitations: It provides review guidance and does not run tests or static analyzers by itself.
It depends on the code and context supplied in the conversation.
It cannot guarantee that every vulnerability or defect will be found.
It may need project-specific standards to judge style and architecture accurately.

use_cases.title: Review a Pull Request Before Merge
use_cases.description: Identify correctness, security, and maintainability concerns before code reaches the main branch.

use_cases.title: Improve Implementation Quality
use_cases.description: Get structured feedback on a new feature, refactor, or bug fix before requesting human review.

use_cases.title: Standardize Team Review Criteria
use_cases.description: Apply a repeatable review checklist across security, performance, testing, and documentation.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Code Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this when sharing a small function or file.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code for readability, correctness, and obvious bugs. Summarize strengths, then list issues by severity with clear fixes.

prompt_templates.title: Pull Request Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this before merging a branch.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this pull request as if you are a senior engineer. Focus on behavior changes, maintainability, tests, and documentation gaps.

prompt_templates.title: Security-Focused Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this for authentication, payments, data handling, or user input changes.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this code for security risks. Check input validation, authorization, injection risks, sensitive data handling, and safe error handling.

prompt_templates.title: Architecture and Performance Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use this for larger changes or shared modules.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this implementation for architecture, performance, and long-term maintainability. Call out tradeoffs, hidden coupling, scalability risks, and missing tests.

output_examples.input: Review a controller method that builds a database query from a request parameter.
output_examples.output: The review flags unsafe query construction as critical, recommends parameterized queries, and asks for tests covering invalid input.

output_examples.input: Review a pull request that adds caching to a service method.
output_examples.output: The review checks cache invalidation, error handling, test coverage, and whether stale data could affect users.

output_examples.input: Review a refactor that splits one large function into several helpers.
output_examples.output: The review evaluates naming, reduced complexity, behavior preservation, and whether the new helper boundaries are clear.

best_practices: Provide the relevant diff, surrounding files, and expected behavior.
State the review focus, such as security, performance, tests, or maintainability.
Share project conventions so feedback matches the team standard.
anti_patterns: Do not ask for a final approval without supplying enough code context.
Do not treat the review as a replacement for tests, builds, or security scanners.
Do not ignore severity labels when planning follow-up work.

faq.question: What does this skill review?
faq.answer: It reviews code quality, best practices, security, performance, tests, and documentation.

faq.question: Can it review pull requests?
faq.answer: Yes. Provide the diff, related context, and the intended behavior for better results.

faq.question: Does it run code or tests?
faq.answer: No. The skill gives review guidance and recommendations based on the provided code.

faq.question: Can it find security vulnerabilities?
faq.answer: It can identify common vulnerability patterns, but it should complement dedicated security tools.

faq.question: Which tools does it support?
faq.answer: It is listed for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: What context should I provide?
faq.answer: Provide the code, expected behavior, relevant tests, and any project standards.
